meta.description: US ski resorts with key features and coordinates. Selection emphasizes major destination resorts; coverage of small/community hills is partial. Compiled April 2026.
sources: OnTheSnow.com / Wikipedia (trail counts, lifts, snowfall via search)
Geographic coordinates from public records and Wikipedia

resorts.name: Aspen Snowmass
resorts.note: Snowmass mountain only; Aspen complex has 4 mountains totaling 5,547 acres

resorts.name: Eldora
resorts.note: Closest major resort to Denver/Boulder

resorts.name: Howelsen Hill
resorts.note: Oldest continuously operating ski area in North America (1915)

resorts.name: Silverton Mountain
resorts.note: Single chairlift, expert-only, mostly guided

resorts.name: Park City Mountain
resorts.note: Combined Park City + Canyons since 2015 connection

resorts.name: Deer Valley
resorts.note: After 2024-25 expansion; ski-only, no snowboards

resorts.name: Alta
resorts.note: Ski-only, no snowboards

resorts.name: Powder Mountain
resorts.note: Lift-served acres only; total acreage including hike-to and cat is ~8,500

resorts.name: Lookout Pass
resorts.note: On ID/MT border

resorts.name: Sunrise Park Resort
resorts.note: Owned by White Mountain Apache Tribe

resorts.name: Palisades Tahoe
resorts.note: 2021 merger of Squaw Valley and Alpine Meadows

resorts.name: Heavenly
resorts.note: CA/NV border resort

resorts.name: Alpine Meadows
resorts.note: Now part of Palisades Tahoe but historically separate

resorts.name: Mountain High
resorts.note: Closest major resort to Los Angeles

resorts.name: Bear Mountain
resorts.note: Big Bear area; Southern California

resorts.name: Snow Summit
resorts.note: Big Bear area; Southern California

resorts.name: Lee Canyon
resorts.note: Closest major resort to Las Vegas

resorts.name: Summit at Snoqualmie
resorts.note: Combined Alpental + Summit West/Central/East

resorts.name: Eaglecrest
resorts.note: Juneau community ski area

resorts.name: Hilltop Ski Area
resorts.note: Anchorage urban ski area

resorts.name: Mad River Glen
resorts.note: Ski-only, no snowboards; cooperatively owned

resorts.name: Magic Mountain (VT)
resorts.note: Independent, throwback to 1970s skiing; cult favorite

resorts.name: Burke
resorts.note: Burke Mountain Academy, top US ski racing academy

resorts.name: Middlebury Snow Bowl
resorts.note: Owned by Middlebury College

resorts.name: Suicide Six
resorts.note: Site of first US ski tow (1934)

resorts.name: Dartmouth Skiway
resorts.note: Owned by Dartmouth College

resorts.name: Camden Snow Bowl
resorts.note: Coastal Maine, ocean views

resorts.name: Whiteface
resorts.note: 1980 Olympics venue; highest vertical in the East

resorts.name: Holiday Valley
resorts.note: Largest ski area in Western NY; lake-effect snowbelt; ~500K visits/yr

resorts.name: Plattekill
resorts.note: Independent, weekends only, expert reputation

resorts.name: Bristol Mountain
resorts.note: Highest vertical between Rockies and Adirondacks

resorts.name: Catamount
resorts.note: On NY/MA border

resorts.name: Blue Mountain
resorts.note: PA's highest vertical drop; ~75 mi NW of Philadelphia; ~300K visits/yr; 100% snowmaking

resorts.name: Camelback
resorts.note: Largest in the Poconos; serves NYC + Philly day-trip market

resorts.name: Whitetail
resorts.note: Serves DC/Baltimore market

resorts.name: Seven Springs
resorts.note: Largest in western PA; serves Pittsburgh

resorts.name: Mountain Creek
resorts.note: Closest major resort to NYC; serves NYC metro area

resorts.name: Berkshire East
resorts.note: Powered by on-site solar + wind

resorts.name: Mohawk Mountain
resorts.note: First snowmaking ski resort in the world (1949)

resorts.name: Buck Hill
resorts.note: Where Lindsey Vonn learned to ski

resorts.name: Mt. Bohemia
resorts.note: Expert-only, Upper Peninsula; no grooming, ungroomed terrain

resorts.name: App Ski Mountain
resorts.note: Appalachian Ski Mountain; near Boone NC

resorts.name: Massanutten
resorts.note: Largest in VA; serves DC market

resorts.name: Wisp
resorts.note: Maryland's only commercial ski resort
